assertions.reason: A budget-capped live SDK probe created a chat completion with `store=true`, but retrieval never succeeded through the SDK user surface: the first retrieve returned `Api(NotFound)` and four later retries returned `Parse` (`invalid type: null, expected a sequence at line 1 column 621`). The same probe could still delete the stored completion successfully, so stored CRUD does not behave correctly end-to-end.

The mocked cargo tests already proved legacy `/v1/completions` create/stream behavior, `text_completion` shape parity, and `best_of`+`stream` rejection; a supplemental compile probe against the public crate API confirmed `client.completions()` exposes no `retrieve` method (`no method named `retrieve` found for reference `&Completions``), which closes the remaining create-only surface gap.

commands.observation: The command intentionally expected compilation to fail; the captured diagnostics include `no method named `retrieve` found for reference `&Completions` in the current scope`, confirming the legacy completions namespace does not expose retrieve-style CRUD methods.

commands.command: set -a && . /Users/staticpayload/Mainframe/openai-rust/creds.txt && set +a && unset OPENAI_BASE_URL && env CARGO_TARGET_DIR="/Users/staticpayload/Mainframe/openai-rust/.factory/validation/compatibility-core/user-testing/target/compat-chat-supplemental" cargo run --quiet --manifest-path "<temporary compat_chat_live_retry_probe Cargo.toml>" > "/Users/staticpayload/.factory/missions/beaa247f-5975-4da4-acc2-19deef388841/evidence/compatibility-core/compat-chat-supplemental/chat_store_live_retry_probe.txt" 2>&1
commands.observation: The live probe created `chatcmpl-DRPLa1LdGIrxP2yI9WidxHpY1qCGk` (`stored_create_request_id=req_64fa66e5aba94a97b880be09992c3daa`), but retrieve attempt 1 returned `Api(NotFound)` and attempts 2-5 returned the same parse error `invalid type: null, expected a sequence at line 1 column 621`; `list_count=0` for the tagged filter and `delete_ok=true` showed deletion still worked.

frictions: The live stored-chat probe showed eventual-consistency-like behavior: the first retrieve returned 404, then subsequent retries returned a stable parse error instead of a successful stored completion payload.
summary: Supplemental validation closed the VAL-COMPAT-005 create-only gap and found a real end-to-end failure for VAL-COMPAT-002 on the live stored-chat retrieval path.
